rationale: The citing paper uses De Angelis et al. (2012) in its core theory section, not just in a background list. It cites that study to define WOM transmission/retransmission and to support the self-enhancement mechanism behind why recipients may retransmit valenced WOM, including secondhand negative stories. Those claims are then used to motivate the paper's expectations about retransmission intentions, indicating a meaningful conceptual role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds its retransmission framing on De Angelis et al.'s self-enhancement account of WOM transmission and its evidence that secondhand negative WOM can be shared for self-presentational reasons. This prior work serves as a key conceptual basis for the citing paper's hypotheses about how WOM valence relates to retransmission intentions.
